extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won Stage 5 sprint after MERLIER Tim's leadout for CAVENDISH clashed with DE BUYST Jasper leading MERLIER Tim on opposite side.
tactical_patterns: DECEUNINCK-QUICKSTEP split leadout across full road width disrupts rival trains positioning, MERLIER Mikhail to CAVENDISH left side
EVENEPOEL Remco gained GC buffer in Golden Kilometer sprint between two towns, not just TT, moving fifth overall
LOTTO-SOUDAL train attempted third consecutive stage win but failed when EWAN Caleb boxed by dual-side Quick-Step positioning

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: 151st career win shows Quick-Step willing to sacrifice Merlier Tim's stage hunt to service senior sprinter
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: gearing malfunction at critical moment but also clearly frustrated being sacrificed for teammate rather than sprinting himself
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: won two prior stages but Quick-Step's split-road leadout tactics denied hat-trick despite having strongest train
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: defended title by using intermediate Golden Kilometer to pad GC, not relying solely on TT time gaps
rider_signals.DE BUYST Jasper: neutral: physical post-sprint confrontation with MERLIER Tim suggests Quick-Step's dual leadout encroached rival space
